mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and Kubernetes resources. It is particularly useful for maintaining security compliance in CI/CD pipelines and production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for proper RBAC configuration and potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists the pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify they are running.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for the Trivy Operator to scan images.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    name: example-app
    kind: Deployment
EOF

```

> Creates a VulnerabilityReport CR for scanning.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    name: example-app
    kind: Deployment
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks if the created Custom Resource is ready and has scanned the specified resource.

```bash

kubectl get vulnerabilityreports -n default

```

> Lists the VulnerabilityReports in the default namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all vulnerability reports and configurations associated with the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup any important configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json > trivy-backup.json

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup current pod configurations.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json > trivy-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Kubernetes API server's certificate is not trusted by your local environment.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly configured with the right context and certificates.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD: CustomResourceDefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io "vulnerabilityreports.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io" is invalid'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs if the CRD already exists or is in an invalid state.

**Fix:**
Delete the existing CRD with 'kubectl delete crd vulnerabilityreports.trivy.aquasecurity.github.io' and re-run the installation.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the configuration or a missing dependency.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace.'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that no resources were found to scan.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the resources you want to scan exist in the specified namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vulnerability scanning features and enhanced reporting capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace and the created VulnerabilityReport should be listed in the default namespace.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    name: example-app
    kind: Deployment
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
